pom.xml.general_idea: Update the project's target Java version property from 17 to 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile and run against Java 17 semantics, failing to utilize Java 21 features or enforce Java 21 compatibility.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated source/target compiler configuration with the modern release flag.
pom.xml.why_exists: The source and target options are deprecated in newer JDKs and Maven compiler plugin versions; using release is required for correct cross-compilation and module path handling in Java 21.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add release flag to the test compiler configuration to ensure tests compile against Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this, tests may compile against an older bytecode version or fail if the plugin defaults are incompatible with the target JDK 21 environment.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for correct serialization metadata in modern Java.

src/test/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/test/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for correct serialization metadata in modern Java.
